perturbation.original_text: Each party shall deduct such taxes from the payments due to the other party hereunder as required by law including withholding taxes (unless an exemption is provided), and shall promptly furnish such other party with appropriate tax receipts. Each party will be solely responsible for any and all taxes imposed thereon, including, without limitation, all income taxes, sales taxes, goods and services taxes. Israel value added tax shall be added, if applicable, to all amounts payable hereunder and will be paid against submission of appropriate tax invoices.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party shall deduct such taxes from the payments due to the other party hereunder as required by law including withholding taxes (unless an exemption is provided), and shall promptly furnish such other party with appropriate tax receipts. NICE will be solely responsible for any and all taxes imposed thereon, including, without limitation, all income taxes, sales taxes, goods and services taxes. Israel value added tax shall be added, if applicable, to all amounts payable hereunder and will be paid against submission of appropriate tax invoices.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that only NICE is responsible for all taxes, while the initial sentence implies both parties share this responsibility or are individually responsible for their own taxes related to the agreement, thus creating uncertainty and conflicting obligations. This contradicts earlier provision that each party deduct taxes from payments due to the other.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party shall deduct such taxes from the payments due to the other party hereunder as required by law including withholding taxes (unless an exemption is provided), and shall promptly furnish such other party with appropriate tax receipts. NICE will be solely responsible for any and all taxes imposed thereon, including, without limitation, all income taxes, sales taxes, goods and services taxes. Israel value added tax shall be added, if applicable, to all amounts payable hereunder and will be paid against submission of appropriate tax invoices.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states "NICE will be solely responsible for any and all taxes imposed thereon", while the contradicted text, which is identical to the changed text, contains the phrase "Each party shall deduct such taxes from the payments due to the other party hereunder as required by law". This creates a direct contradiction because the first statement implies only NICE is responsible for all taxes, whereas the second statement implies that both parties have a responsibility to deduct taxes from payments due to the other. This alteration introduces an ambiguity regarding tax responsibilities, making the text inconsistent and leading to uncertainty about the obligations of each party.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained herein or otherwise, Contractor's liability to NICE for any indirect, special, incidental, exemplary or consequential damages as a result of any claim arising under this Agreement or in connection therewith, regardless of whether Contractor has been advised of the possibility of such damages, shall not exceed five million US dollars ($5,000,000) in the aggregate for all claims, except for infringement of Intellectual Property rights for which Contractor is liable under Section 12.6. This Section shall not be construed or used in the construction of this Agreement as imposing on Contractor any liability for which it is not otherwise liable. Further, this Section shall not be construed as derogating from any applicable law which cannot be changed or waived by contract.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained herein or otherwise, Contractor's liability to NICE for any indirect, special, incidental, exemplary or consequential damages as a result of any claim arising under this Agreement or in connection therewith, regardless of whether Contractor has been advised of the possibility of such damages, shall not exceed five million US dollars ($5,000,000) in the aggregate for all claims, including infringement of Intellectual Property rights for which Contractor is liable under Section 12.6. This Section shall not be construed or used in the construction of this Agreement as imposing on Contractor any liability for which it is not otherwise liable. Further, this Section shall not be construed as derogating from any applicable law which cannot be changed or waived by contract.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation modifies Section 15.4 to remove the exception for Intellectual Property rights infringement from the liability cap. This creates an in-text contradiction. Initially, the contract specified that the $5,000,000 cap did not apply to IP infringements, implying unlimited liability in those cases. With this change, IP infringements are now subject to the $5,000,000 cap. This contradicts Section 12.6, which outlines Contractor's indemnification obligations for IP rights infringement. This change will leave the parties in conflict as Section 12.6 implies unlimited liability for IP infringements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Contractor shall indemnify, hold harmless and defend NICE from and against any and all damages (including all damages awarded to a third party and payable by NICE), costs, losses, and expenses (including settlement awards and reasonable attorney's fees) arising from any claim or suit made against NICE or a third party which NICE is obligated to indemnify, by a third party based on the allegation that the Products infringe or violate any Intellectual Property right due to the components and/or sub assemblies supplied by Contractor hereunder and/or the manufacturing processes and methods as performed by Contractor hereunder. Contractor shall include in all its agreements with suppliers/ manufacturers provisions regarding Intellectual Property indemnification substantially similar to those included in this Agreement, providing inter alia that they are freely assignable to NICE without any modification or consent.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 15.4 created an exception for intellectual property rights infringement from the liability cap. The changed text removes this exception, subjecting IP infringements to the $5,000,000 cap. This directly contradicts Section 12.6, which states that the Contractor shall indemnify NICE from any damages arising from IP infringement claims, implying potentially unlimited liability. The change creates an in-text contradiction by capping liability in a section where previously there was not.

perturbation.original_text: NICE shall only be entitled to setoff any amount due to Contractor against any amount due from Contractor to NICE, if the setoff amount is: (i) due according to this Agreement and is subject to an invoice lawfully issued hereunder; or (ii) liquidated damages according to this Agreement. In the event of setoff, Contractor shall receive, at the same time, a written notice signed by vice president operations of NICE detailing the setoff. Contractor shall not be entitled to setoff any amount due to NICE from Contractor hereunder against any amount due from NICE to Contractor according to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: NICE shall be entitled to setoff any amount due to Contractor against any amount due from Contractor to NICE, if the setoff amount is: (i) due according to this Agreement and is subject to an invoice lawfully issued hereunder; or (ii) any reason including liquidated damages as per NICE discretion. In the event of setoff, Contractor shall receive, at the same time, a written notice signed by vice president operations of NICE detailing the setoff. Contractor shall not be entitled to setoff any amount due to NICE from Contractor hereunder against any amount due from NICE to Contractor according to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the conditions under which NICE can set off amounts due to the Contractor, this creates a contradiction. Previously, setoffs were limited to amounts due under the agreement or for liquidated damages. Now, NICE can set off for 'any reason' at its discretion. The conflict arises because the last sentence remains unchanged: 'Contractor shall not be entitled to setoff any amount due to NICE from Contractor hereunder against any amount due from NICE to Contractor according to this Agreement.' This creates an imbalanced and contradictory clause, where NICE has broad setoff rights, but the Contractor has none, despite owing due amounts.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NICE shall be entitled to setoff any amount due to Contractor against any amount due from Contractor to NICE, if the setoff amount is: (i) due according to this Agreement and is subject to an invoice lawfully issued hereunder; or (ii) any reason including liquidated damages as per NICE discretion. In the event of setoff, Contractor shall receive, at the same time, a written notice signed by vice president operations of NICE detailing the setoff. Contractor shall not be entitled to setoff any amount due to NICE from Contractor hereunder against any amount due from NICE to Contractor according to this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the conditions under which NICE can set off amounts due to the Contractor. Originally, setoffs were limited to amounts due under the agreement or for liquidated damages. Now, NICE can set off for 'any reason' at its discretion, while the Contractor's setoff rights remain limited, creating a clear imbalance and contradiction within the same section. The last sentence, 'Contractor shall not be entitled to setoff any amount due to NICE from Contractor hereunder against any amount due from NICE to Contractor according to this Agreement,' directly conflicts with NICE's broadened setoff rights.
